summary: Dependency-confusion squat of @k18n scope with auto-executing postinstall (current payload benign)
details: Package publishes under the @k18n npm scope at version 99.0.2 with a postinstall hook (scripts.postinstall = "node index.js") that auto-executes on install. The README describes @k18n as an internal Kuaishou namespace, and the abnormally high version (99.0.2) is consistent with dependency-confusion squat shape: any installer whose package manager resolves @k18n against the public registry instead of the intended internal registry would execute the package's index.js at install time. The current index.js payload only prints a proof-of-concept console message — it does not exfiltrate data, fetch remote code, or persist. There is no concrete installer-harm code in this version, only the namespace-squat-plus-auto-execute primitive. Routing to human review so a reviewer can confirm scope ownership/attribution with Kuaishou and decide whether to take down the public publication.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
